summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the sermon by describing the 'midnight' season of life—a dark, confusing, and often painful period where God's promises seem distant and prayers feel unanswered. He acknowledged his own two-and-a-half-year journey through such a season, offering empathy and encouragement. The core message, titled 'Every Miracle Has a Midnight,' asserts that God does His best work in our darkest moments. Pastor Travis taught that midnight is not the absence of God's movement but the birthing place of miracles, breakthroughs, and resurrections. He provided three directives for navigating these seasons: first, do not lose hope, anchoring ourselves in God's trustworthy character and the truth that the longer the process, the bigger the promise. Second, do not lose faith, which is the confident proof in what God has said, even when it's invisible. He illustrated this with the analogy of a seed that must be buried, broken, and develop roots in the dark before any fruit is visible. Third, do not stop praying and praising, using the example of Paul and Silas, whose worship in a prison at midnight precipitated a miraculous earthquake that set everyone free. The sermon concluded with a call to persistent prayer, communal agreement in faith, and an altar call for salvation and specific miracles, reinforcing that God's timing is perfect and His ways are higher than ours.
mainPoints: Do not lose hope, because God is a trustworthy promise-keeper and the longer the process often signals a bigger promise.
Do not lose faith, which is the confident proof in God's word and character, believing for the invisible work He is doing in the 'midnight' season.
Do not stop praying and praising, as worship in our darkest hour can be the catalyst for God's miraculous intervention, setting both us and others free.

versesMentioned.text: Let us hold unswervingly to the hope we profess, for he who promised is faithful.
For I know the plans I have for you,” declares the Lord, “plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.
God is not human, that he should lie, not a human being, that he should change his mind. Does he speak and then not act? Does he promise and not fulfill?
But if we hope for what we do not yet have, we wait for it patiently.
Now faith is confidence in what we hope for and assurance about what we do not see.

Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. Test me in this,” says the Lord Almighty, “and see if I will not throw open the floodgates of heaven and pour out so much blessing that there will not be room enough to store it.
Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
“For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways,” declares the Lord.
The least of you will become a thousand, the smallest a mighty nation. I am the Lord; in its time I will do this swiftly.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.

About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them. Suddenly there was such a violent earthquake that the foundations of the prison were shaken. At once all the prison doors flew open, and everyone’s chains came loose.
Therefore confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.
Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.
Therefore I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
Again, truly I tell you that if two of you on earth agree about anything they ask for, it will be done for them by my Father in heaven. For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them.

Monday.scripture: Hebrews 10:23 (NIV) - Let us hold unswervingly to the hope we profess, for he who promised is faithful.
Monday.action: Write down one specific promise from God you are holding onto. Place it somewhere you will see it throughout the day.
Monday.prayer: God, thank you that you are faithful. Help me to hold tightly to the hope I have in your promises today.

Tuesday.scripture: Hebrews 11:1 (NIV) - Now faith is confidence in what we hope for and assurance about what we do not see.
Tuesday.action: Identify one area where you see no change yet. Speak a declaration of faith over it, thanking God that He is at work.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, I choose to have confidence in what I hope for. Strengthen my faith to believe you are working, even when I cannot see it.

Wednesday.scripture: Acts 16:25-26 (NIV) - About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God... Suddenly there was such a violent earthquake...
Wednesday.action: In a challenging moment today, choose to listen to or sing a worship song instead of voicing a complaint.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, teach me to worship you in the middle of my difficulty. Let my praise be a catalyst for your power in my life.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 55:8 (NIV) - “For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways,” declares the Lord.
Thursday.action: When you feel confused by a situation, pause and pray, 'God, I trust that your thoughts and ways are higher than mine in this.'
Thursday.prayer: Father, I surrender my need to understand everything. I choose to trust that your perspective and plan are perfect.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 18:19-20 (NIV) - ...if two of you on earth agree about anything they ask for, it will be done...
Friday.action: Text or call a trusted Christian friend and ask them to agree with you in prayer for one specific need.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for the power of agreement. As we gather in your name, we believe for your will to be done in this situation.

Saturday.scripture: Philippians 4:6 (NIV) - Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.
Saturday.action: Take 5 minutes of quiet. Write down every worry that comes to mind, then pray and surrender each one to God with thanksgiving.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I bring all my anxieties to you. I thank you for your peace and for hearing every one of my requests.
